# Backend Frameworks

## Preferred Options
### FastAPI
Status: Approved and preferred for Python-based backend services

Strengths:
- strong typing and schema support
- excellent API ergonomics
- automatic OpenAPI generation
- strong fit for workflow and data-oriented systems

Use when:
- the team is comfortable in Python
- APIs and integrations are central
- the system benefits from explicit data models

### Node.js
Status: Approved and preferred for JavaScript or TypeScript-based backend services

Strengths:
- consistent language across frontend and backend
- strong ecosystem
- good fit for realtime and web-heavy applications

Use when:
- frontend and backend are delivered by the same team
- JavaScript or TypeScript is the dominant skill set
- shared code or shared validation models matter

## Guidance
Both FastAPI and Node.js are approved defaults. The team should choose the one that best matches skills, maintainability, and adjacent system needs.
